inputs.price_source: Yahoo Finance HIMS regular market price 2026-07-18
inputs.shares_source: ~252M diluted shares implied from FY2025 net income $128.4M / diluted EPS $0.51 (10-K FY2025)
inputs.fcf_source: Normalized owner cash per share after sustained brand marketing and pharmacy/peptide facility build-out; FY2025 OCF $300M ($1.19/sh) minus growth capex and recurring customer-acquisition drag — not raw operating cash flow (10-K FY2025 cash flow statement; PaymentsToAcquireOtherProductiveAssets $226M)
inputs.cash_source: Cash and equivalents $222.3M at March 31, 2026 (10-Q Q1 2026)
inputs.investment_securities_source: Available-for-sale debt securities $348.9M current + $351.3M noncurrent at December 31, 2025 (10-K FY2025)
inputs.convertible_debt_source: Convertible senior notes $974.1M noncurrent at March 31, 2026; ~$970M proceeds issued FY2025 (10-Q Q1 2026; 10-K FY2025)
inputs.telehealth_consultations_source: Over fifty million telehealth consultations since founding (10-K FY2025 Item 1)
inputs.fda_glp1_risk: February 2026 FDA statement named Hims & Hers regarding restrictions on mass-marketed compounded GLP-1 semaglutide alternatives (10-K FY2025 Risk Factors)
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence starting owner cash $0.80/sh haircuts FY2025 OCF for DTC marketing reinvestment, $226M productive-asset spend, and regulatory optionality on compounded GLP-1 weight-loss revenue.

marvin_floor.notes: Normalized owner cash after marketing and capex haircut; filings only for revenue, OCF, debt

blended_best.notes: Marvin floor only; no approved third-party blend

bear.notes: FDA restricts compounded GLP-1 semaglutide; customer acquisition costs rise; Q1 2026 revenue growth already slowed to ~4% YoY

base.notes: Core telehealth categories grow; weight-loss mix shifts to branded alternatives and labs; operating margin holds near FY2025 levels

bull.notes: 503B/peptide facilities scale; international markets contribute; GLP-1 compounding path preserved with formulary changes

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Goodwill and intangibles $196M from acquisitions; $700M debt securities at fair value on balance sheet vs operating platform value (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Consolidated operating income positive FY2025 $105.6M and Q1 2026 $78.3M (10-K, 10-Q)

option_scan.question: Backlog / RPO not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Subscription telehealth model; revenue recognized as services delivered, not long-dated backlog

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: Investment portfolio is liquid AFS debt securities, not private stakes (10-K)

option_scan.question: Embedded product option in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: 503B compounding and peptide manufacturing already in revenue path; GLP-1 weight-loss is embedded product line, not separate terminal (10-K Item 1)

optionality_gate.notes: Cash $222M + securities $700M ≈ $922M / 252M ≈ $3.66/sh is balance-sheet liquidity, not normalized owner cash; GLP-1 regulatory tail can impair revenue faster than cash floor

stance_proposal.override_reason: FY2025 scale real (revenue $2.35B, OCF $300M) but moat eroding on FDA compounded GLP-1 scrutiny, Q1 2026 revenue growth ~4%, and $974M convertible debt. Lawrence base IRR ~0.7% at $32.84 on $0.80/sh normalized owner cash.

growth_explanation.base_growth_mechanism: Telehealth subscriber expansion in hair, sexual health, mental health, and labs; weight-loss mix depends on GLP-1 regulatory path
growth_explanation.bear_falsifier: FDA removes compounded semaglutide revenue; marketing costs rise with flat subscribers
growth_explanation.bull_falsifier: International roll-out and 503B/peptide capacity lift margins without regulatory setback
